Dataset_Description: Alloy Data Extraction from provided paper
Paper_Title: Development of processing strategies for multigraded selective laser melting of Ti6Al4V and IN718

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of Ti6Al4V gas atomized powder (LPW) on Ti6Al4V substrate using Powderful prototype. Layer thickness 50 um, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Optimal energy density for full densification: E >= 160 J/mm3. Sample shown in Fig. 10 produced at E = 178 J/mm3.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Fine acicular alpha prime (alpha') martensitic laths organized within prior beta grain boundaries with different inclinations, mainly at +/-45 degrees with respect to the build direction. Columnar grains result from epitaxial growth of the previous layer upon successive laser scans. Some lack of fusion defects present.

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of IN718 gas atomized powder (LPW) on IN718 substrate using Powderful prototype. Layer thickness 50 um, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Optimal energy density for full densification: E >= 300 J/mm3. Sample shown in Fig. 11 produced at E = 300 J/mm3.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Typical layer-by-layer SLM manufacturing characteristics with visible melt pool morphology. Columnar grains across several layers with very fine dendritic structure. Strong segregation in interdendritic zones enriched in Nb and Mo. Bright phases in BSE image achieve Nb content up to 23.7 wt% (EDX) and Mo up to 2.5 wt%, associated with Ni3Nb delta-phase formation.

Materials._description: --- Material 3: Ti6Al4V + 10 wt% IN718 blend SLM ---

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of Ti6Al4V + 10 wt% IN718 premixed blend using Powderful multi-material system with double hopper and mixing chamber. Layer thickness 50 um, hatch 50 um, Ar atmosphere. Produced at E = 165 J/mm3. Pore-free and crack-free.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Non-uniform microstructure across specimen surface. Deposited layers clearly distinguishable, no alpha martensitic laths observed. IN718 alloying elements (Ni, Cr, Fe) act as beta-stabilizers for titanium, promoting beta phase growth and retention to room temperature. Dendritic microstructure not clearly observable, indicating low microsegregation. Macrosegregated areas at layer interfaces with Ni up to 15 wt% and Cr up to 3.6 wt%.

Materials._description: --- Material 4: Ti6Al4V + 20 wt% IN718 blend SLM ---

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of Ti6Al4V + 20 wt% IN718 premixed blend using Powderful multi-material system. Layer thickness 50 um, hatch 50 um, Ar atmosphere. Produced at E = 267 J/mm3. Crack-free for E > 266 J/mm3. Highest solidification range (delta T = 288 C) but still processable.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Complex yet uniform microstructure with clear evidence of a second phase nucleated in the interdendritic zones. Beta phase formation expected with increased IN718 content. SEM/EDX analysis shows second phase rich in Ni (up to 17 wt%). Solidification range 288 C, the highest among all blends.

Materials._description: --- Material 5: Ti6Al4V + 30 wt% IN718 blend SLM (cracked) ---

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of Ti6Al4V + 30 wt% IN718 premixed blend using Powderful multi-material system. Layer thickness 50 um, hatch 50 um, Ar atmosphere. Produced at E = 400 J/mm3. Severe macro cracks regardless of process parameters. Cracks comparable to specimen size.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Intermetallic Ti2Ni phase nucleates (bright phase). Alpha-Ti + Ti2Ni eutectoid transformation starts at beta grain boundaries. Massive precipitation of intermetallic phase causes dendritic solidification microstructure to be no longer observable. Ti2Ni starts to form in the liquid phase at this composition. Solidification range 194 C.

Materials._description: --- Material 6: Ti6Al4V + 40 wt% IN718 blend SLM (cracked) ---

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: SLM of Ti6Al4V + 40 wt% IN718 premixed blend using Powderful multi-material system. Layer thickness 50 um, hatch 50 um, Ar atmosphere. Produced at E = 400 J/mm3. Severe macro cracks regardless of process parameters. Ti2Ni intermetallic forms in liquid state.
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Intermetallic Ti2Ni phase present (bright phase). Alpha-Ti + Ti2Ni eutectoid transformation at beta grain boundaries. Massive precipitation of intermetallic phase, dendritic solidification microstructure no longer observable. Solidification range 119 C. Alpha phase destabilized with temperature formation decreased below 600 K.

Process_Info.Process_Text_For_AI: Multigraded Ti6Al4V/IN718 specimen built via multi-material SLM on Ti6Al4V substrate. First 42 layers pure Ti6Al4V, then discrete steps of 5 wt% IN718 increase (5, 10, 15, 20 wt%), 12 layers per blend. Two replicates. Layer interdiffusion creates smoother composition transition than nominal. Fully dense and crack-free up to 20 wt% IN718. Hardness increases gradually along build direction due to dilution effect and increased hard intermetallic inclusions.
Process_Info.Main_Phase: alpha prime martensite (base) to beta + intermetallic (top)
Process_Info.Microstructure_Text_For_AI: Graded microstructure along build direction. Base region shows Ti6Al4V-like alpha prime martensitic structure. Increasing IN718 content promotes beta phase stabilization and formation of hard intermetallic inclusions (Ti2Ni). Dilution effect from layer interdiffusion creates smoother composition and hardness transition than premixed blends. Highest hardness in layers enriched in Ni, Nb, Cr and Fe.
